data.narrative:
Nabla Bio is a Series A biotech company founded by Surge Biswas and Frances Anastassacos. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 11 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health, including peers such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B Seed), and Neko Health ($1.8B Series B).

Two methodologies were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $37M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $74M. Peer-median analysis across seven same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $810M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comparable anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.1x across three biotech tickers) was not applicable due to Nabla's undisclosed revenue.

The triangulation of these two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $63M to $931M, with a median of $442M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate comparables; peer valuations in the Biotech & Health sector at proximate stages vary substantially. Key uncertainties include partnership value realization from the reported $1.5B+ partnerships and the appropriate discount or premium relative to the observed peer set. The stage-progression floor ($74M) and peer median ($810M) anchor the likely range but do not resolve which peer cohort best reflects Nabla's risk and upside profile.
